title: The Nation – July - August 2026 - Part 217
content:
Shame, yet my skin stings as the word floods my face, a feast of mosquitos.

the Trail of Tears. I am fourteen hundred miles from a sign at the gate that says collection of poems believes his gospel, like a missionary to the Seminoles in the Everglades before 2021. His latest from a guard by the name of Martínez who brawled his job away and still book                                                          Floaters in I am fourteen hundred miles from Alligator Alcatraz, fourteen hundred miles Poetry for his for the alligator to slither from the patch and drag him into the underworld.

waits for the Reaper to stride from the patch and handcuff him in the hallway, won the National palpitating when reporters speak of patches worn by the guards. He still Martín Espada Ana’s husband testifies, freed in court by writ of habeas corpus, his heart They tell you they’re your executioners, that you won’t get out of there alive                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   , in the hands of his brother guards. He is the Reaper, the alligator, the skull.

the keeper of keys to the shackles, the guard who slaps the Reaper patch cuts off the flow of blood to the hands, where they have it easy, he says. He is where the rules on the wall say the guards must loosen the shackles if the steel of his grandparents, not the man caged in solitary, the Special Housing Unit, or the orange-picker, not the inmates pleading for water in the Spanish tongue Officer Martínez is not Saul of Tarsus, not the landscaper or the house painter him skin, and he has a vision, flashbacks of the strippers teasing him in Miami.

hops into his car. Are you wearing a wire? he snaps. She lifts her T-shirt to show I have them made, and I give them out. He storms back, still jobless, and Courtney MARTÍ                                                                                                                                               N ESPADA Courtney hears in her head his words about the patch:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  You can just have it.

the job, still in his uniform, another Grim Reaper patch stuck to his shoulder.

An hour later he returns, storming through the gate to snarl at his boss about —Donald Trump, circle back to hoist the sign that reads Shame at the gate of Alligator Alcatraz.

Route 41 is the road to Damascus, if his sun-struck vision will clear and he will up about one percent.” a New Testament conversion reenacted in the swamp, if he is Saul of Tarsus, if line.... Your chances go Officer Martínez rolls away, and the keepers of the vigil wonder if they saw Don’t run in a straight strung between them; the backdrop of cages behind the danse macabre.
